title: frasera albicaulis var. nitida
intro: Frasera albicaulis var. nitida, also known as the white frasera, is a variant of the white fringed sage plant, a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of North America. Traditionally, the roots of the white fringed sage have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Frasera albicaulis var. nitida include alleviating symptoms of colds and flu, soothing digestive issues, and reducing inflammation and pain. Frasera albicaulis var. nitida can be prepared as a tea by steeping the roots in hot water, or it can be made into a tincture or infused oil for topical application.
